remember: Students will be able to recall the various secondary activities such as agriculture, animal husbandry, forestry, and fishing.
Students will be able to identify the different types of secondary activities and their importance in a region.
Students will be able to list the various secondary activities practiced in different parts of the world.
understand: Students will be able to understand the relationship between secondary activities and the environment.
Students will be able to comprehend the impact of secondary activities on the economy and society.
Students will be able to analyze the role of secondary activities in the development of a region.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of secondary activities to understand the development of a region.
Students will be able to use the concept of secondary activities to analyze the impact of human activities on the environment.
Students will be able to apply the principles of sustainable development to secondary activities.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the advantages and disadvantages of different secondary activities.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the different types of secondary activities.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of secondary activities on the environment and society.

ncert_objectives: Students will be able to understand the various types of secondary activities and their importance in a region.
Students will be able to explain the relationship between secondary activities and the environment.
Students will be able to describe the impact of secondary activities on the economy and society.
board_exam_objectives: Students will be able to explain the concept of secondary activities and their importance in a region.
Students will be able to describe the advantages and disadvantages of different secondary activities.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of secondary activities on the environment and society.
